properties.remark: EYEWITNESS REPORTED ROTATING FUNNEL CLOUD IMPACTING A NEIGHBORS HOME DAMAGING A PORTABLE BOAT SHED AND A FENCE. TIME IS APPROXIMATE. NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED TO THE HOME.
MILLER LAKE CAMPGROUND. LOTS OF HAIL CAUSED TENTS TO COLLAPSE.

AT 2320 REPORTED 1 HAIL...OBSERVER LOCATION RUCH ON SQUIRES PEAK
LA113 SPOTTER, ONE INCH OR BETTER HAIL SPOTTED BETWEEN FORT ROCK AND HIGHWAY 31
QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND LOCALLY HAIL HAS ACCUMULATED UP TO 3 INCHES DEEP.
A SCANNER PICKED UP A REPORT FROM OSP OF GOLFBALL SIZE HAIL AS WELL AS INCHES OF ACCUMULATING HAIL ON THE ROADWAY AT MILEPOST 27 ON HIGHWAY 31.
LARGEST HAIL AT LEAST 1 INCH. HOLES PUNCHED IN PLASTIC GREENHOUSE. SOME AGRICULTURE AND OTHER VEGETATION DAMAGE. HAIL DURATION 10 MINUTES
SLIDES REPORTED ON SALMON RIVER ROAD AND HIGHWAY 96 FROM FORKS COMPLEX BURN AREA. ROADS REPORTED AS BEING DOWN TO ONE LANE. RELAYED FROM THE INCIDENT METEOROLOGIST ON SITE AT THE FORKS COMPLEX.
SLIDES REPORTED ON SALMON RIVER ROAD AND HIGHWAY 96 FROM FORKS COMPLEX BURN AREA. ROADS REPRTED AS BEIN DOWN TO ONE LANE. RELAYED FROM THE INCIDENT METEOROLOGIST ON SITE AT THE FORKS COMPLEX.
SPOTTER REPORTING VISIBILITY REDUCED TO A MILE OR LESS DUE TO BLOWING SAND AND DUST ALONG ROAD FROM DORRIS TO TULELAKE. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 30 MPH WITH HIGHER GUSTS.
SPOTTER REPORTED SUSTAINED WINDS OF 25 MPH WITH GUSTS UP TO 45 MPH AND BLOWING DUST. ZERO VISIBILITY ON OLD STATE HWY AND REDUCED VISIBILITY ON HWY 97 N OF MACDOEL.
